__UsualType.summary: This enum is used to describe the type of USUAL values in the X# Runtime. It is based on the original USUAL type values in the VO runtime and has been extended with some additional type numbers.

__UsualType.Array.summary: The usual contains an ARRAY value

__UsualType.Binary.summary: The usual contains a Binary value (new in X#).

__UsualType.Byte.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Byte values are stored as LONG.

__UsualType.Char.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Char values are stored as LONG

__UsualType.Codeblock.summary: The usual contains an CODEBLOCK value

__UsualType.Currency.summary: The usual contains a Currency value (new in X#).

__UsualType.Date.summary: The usual contains a DATE value

__UsualType.DateTime.summary: The usual contains a DateTime value (new in X#).

__UsualType.Decimal.summary: The usual contains a Decimal value (new in X#).

__UsualType.DWord.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. DWord values are stored as LONG or FLOAT.

__UsualType.Dynamic.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Dynamic values are stored as OBJECT

__UsualType.Fixed.summary: This value is NEVER used for USUALs (this was also defined in VO but never used).

__UsualType.Float.summary: The usual contains a FLOAT value

__UsualType.Int64.summary: The usual contains an INT64 value (new in Vulcan and X#).

__UsualType.Logic.summary: The usual contains an LOGIC value

__UsualType.Long.summary: The usual contains a LONG value

__UsualType.Memo.summary: The usual contains an Memo value. This value is there for compatibility with VO but never used.

__UsualType.Null.summary: The usual contains a DBNull.Value value (new in X#).

__UsualType.Object.summary: The usual contains an OBJECT value

__UsualType.Psz.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual.
The usual contains an PSZ value

__UsualType.Ptr.summary: The usual contains an PTR value

__UsualType.Real4.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Real4 values are stored as FLOAT

__UsualType.Real8.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Real8 values are stored as FLOAT.

__UsualType.ShortInt.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Short values are stored as LONG.

__UsualType.String.summary: The usual contains an STRING value

__UsualType.Symbol.summary: The usual contains an SYMBOL value

__UsualType.UInt64.summary: The usual contains an UINT64 value (new in Vulcan and X#).

__UsualType.Word.summary: This value is in the enum for completeness but never used inside a usual. Word values are stored as LONG.

_C2Hex.summary: Convert a string value to a hexadecimal string.
_C2Hex.param: When TRUE then the inidividual characters are separated with a space in the result string
_C2Hex.returns: A string with the hex representation of the value

_FieldNames.summary: Helper class for VoDbTrans and VoDbSort()

_FieldNames.FieldCount.summary: Number of fields in the list.

_JoinList.Count.summary: Number of fields in the list.

_JoinList.Fields.summary: List of field areas and positions.

_Like.summary: Determine if a string matches a wildcard pattern (like the wildcard pattern for the DIR command in the OS).
_Like.param: The wildcard to use. '*' matches 0 or more characters until the next non-wildcard character, '?' matches any character, all other characters must match exactly.
_Like.remarks: This function is case sensitive. If you want to do a case insensitive compare, use Like()

_Quit.summary: Terminate application processing, close all open files, and return control to the operating system.
_Quit.remarks: This function can be used from anywhere in an application. A RETURN executed from the Start() function can also be used to QUIT an application.

_RddList.summary: Helper structure to store information for a list of RDD names for DbUseArea()

_RddList.uiRddCount.summary: Number of names in the list.

_SplitPath.param: The drive letter followed by a colon.
The directories, including the trailing slash. Forward slashes and backslashes both may be present in <cPath>. Forward slashes (/) are converted to backslashes (\).
The file name, without the extension.
The extension, including the leading period.

ActualTypeAttribute.Value.summary: The actual type of the parameter.

AllTrim.returns: A trimmed string, with leading and trailing spaces removed.

AltD.remarks: This function is inlined by the compiler, but is included so it can be used in Macros as well.

AltD_2.param: This parameter is ignored in X#
AltD_2.remarks: This function is inlined by the compiler, but is included so it can be used in Macros as well.

Ansi2OemA_2.summary: Convert an array of ANSI characters to OEM characters, changing the contents of the argument as well as the return value.
Ansi2OemA_2.param: A byte array that contains the string to convert

Asc.remarks:
The return value of Asc() in XSharp depends on the setting of SetAnsi().
When SetAnsi() = TRUE then the active windows Ansi codepage is used to calculate the result.
When SetAnsi() = FALSE then the active windows Oem codepage is used to calculate the result.
This is different from the behior in most single byte versions of Xbase where Asc() simply returnes the byte value of the 1st character of the string.

AscA.remarks: AscA() always uses the current Ansi codepage, and ignores the setting of SetAnsi()

AscW.summary: Convert a character to its Unicode ASCII value.

B64EncFile.summary: This function is not implemented yet

B64EncString.summary: This function is not implemented yet

BaseIndex.summary: Base Index class. Does not implement anything.

BaseIndex.ctor.param: Workarea object that 'owns' this index object

BaseMemo.summary: Base Memo class. Does not implement anything.

BaseMemo.ctor.param: Workarea object that 'owns' this memo object

C2Hex.summary: Convert a string value to a hexadecimal string.
C2Hex.returns: A string with the hex representation of the value

Chr.remarks:
The value of dwChar must be between 0 and 255
The return value of Chr() in XSharp depends on the setting of SetAnsi().
When SetAnsi() = TRUE then the active windows Ansi codepage is used to calculate the character.
When SetAnsi() = FALSE then the active windows Oem codepage is used to calculate the character.
This is different from the behior in most single byte versions of Xbase where Chr() simply returnes a string with a single byte that matches the number passed to this function.

ChrA.summary: Convert an ASCII code to a character value, always using the Ansi codepage, ignoring the SetAnsi() setting.

ChrW.summary: Convert an ASCII code to a character value.

ClassLibraryAttribute.GlobalClassName.summary: Name of the class where functions, defines and globals are stored.

ClipperCallingConventionAttribute.Parame.summary: List of parameter names for the method or constructor

CollationMode.summary: This enum specifies the collation mode used to create and update index strings.

CompilerVersionAttribute.Version.summary: The version and dialect of the compiler that created the assembly.

ConDateTime.summary: Format a set of numbers representing a year, month, and day as a Date.
ConDateTime.param: A valid year. If the century digits are not specified, the century is determined by the rules of SetEpoch().
A number from 1 through 12 representing a valid month.
A number representing a valid day of dwMonth.
ConDateTime.returns: The date that corresponds to the passed arguments. If any of the arguments specified do not represent a valid year, month, or day, a DateTime.MinValue is returned.

ConTime.summary: Return the timestring from a DateTime structure
ConTime.param: DateTime values that needs to be converted
ConTime.returns: A (military) time that corresponds to the passed arguments in the format HH:MM:SS without AM/PM notation.

CoreDb.summary:
The CoreDb class contains the methods to manipulate Workareas.
The class is stateless. The Workarea state is stored in the RuntimeState.

CoreDb._ErrInfoPtr.summary: Return exception object from last RDD operation.

CoreDb.Alias.summary: Return the alias of a specified work area as a string.

remarks.note: Alias(), VoDbAlias() and CoreDb.Alias() are aliases

CoreDb.Append.remarks:
This function is like DBAppend() but is strongly typed.
VoDbAppend() and CoreDb.Append() are aliases

CoreDb.BlobInfo.summary: Retrieve information about a memo column.
CoreDb.BlobInfo.param: The returnvalue is returned through this parameter. When set on entry this is the new value of the setting.

CoreDb.Bof.returns: TRUE after an attempt to skip backward beyond the first logical record in a database file or if the current database file contains no records; otherwise, FALSE. If there is no database file open in the current work area, CoreDbBOF() returns TRUE.
CoreDb.Bof.remarks: This function is like BOF(). VoDbBof() and CoreDb.Bof() are aliases

CoreDb.BuffRefresh.summary: Refresh the buffer for the current Workarea, discarding any changes that were made.
CoreDb.BuffRefresh.remarks:
This function is like DBBuffRefresh().
VoDbBuffRefresh() and CoreDb.BuffRefresh() are aliases

CoreDb.ClearFilter.remarks:
This function is like DBClearFilter().
VoDbClearFilter() and CoreDb.ClearFilter() are aliases

CoreDb.ClearLocate.summary: Clear a locate condition by deleting the locate code block.
CoreDb.ClearLocate.remarks:
This function is like DBClearLocate().
VoDbClearLocate() and CoreDb.ClearLocate() are aliases

CoreDb.ClearRelation.remarks:
This function is like DBClearRelation().
VoDbClearRelation() and CoreDb.ClearRelation() are aliases

CoreDb.ClearScope.remarks:
This function is like DBClearScope().
VoDbClearScope() and CoreDb.ClearScope() are aliases

CoreDb.CloseAll.summary: Close all files in all work areas.
CoreDb.CloseAll.remarks:
This function is like DBCloseAll().
VoDbCloseAll() and CoreDb.CloseAll() are aliases

CoreDb.CloseArea.summary: Close all files in a work area.
CoreDb.CloseArea.remarks:
This function is like DbCloseArea().
VoDbCloseArea() and CoreDb.CloseArea() are aliases

CoreDb.Commit.summary: Flush pending updates in one work area.
CoreDb.Commit.remarks:
This function is like DBCommit().
VoDbCommit() and CoreDb.Commit() are aliases

CoreDb.CommitAll.summary: Flush pending updates in all work areas.
CoreDb.CommitAll.remarks:
This function is like DBCommitAll().
VoDbCommitAll() and CoreDb.CommitAll() are aliases

CoreDb.Continue.remarks:
This function is like DBContinue().
VoDbContinue() and CoreDb.Continue() are aliases

overloads.summary: Create new file through the specified RDDs

CoreDb.Create.summary: Create new file through the specified RDDs
CoreDb.Create.param: List of RDDs to use when creating the file
Name of the file to create. When no extension is specified then the default extension for the RDD will be used.
Structure to use when creating the file.
Type of the class that must be used to work with the RDD.
TRUE opens the database file in a new work area (first available). FALSE opens it in the current work area. lNew is useful only when lOpen has a value of TRUE. The default is FALSE.

The alias to be associated with the work area where the file is opened. Within a single thread, X# will not accept duplicate aliases. cAlias is useful only when lOpen has a value of TRUE. The default alias is the filename without extension
The delimiter for fields within a delimited database file. The default is a NULL string
TRUE specifies that the file should remain open after creating. FALSE closes the file.

TRUE specifies that an existing database file be opened. FALSE specifies that that a new database file be opened. The default is FALSE. This can be used to open existing SDF and delimited files, which do not have a structure in the header ? in which case, an empty aStruct should be used.
CoreDb.Create.returns: TRUE when succesfull, otherwise FALSE. When an error has occurred then you can retrieve that error from RuntimeState.LastRddError.

CoreDb.Dbf.summary: Return the name of the alias.
CoreDb.Dbf.remarks: In the FoxPro dialect the full path is returned.

CoreDb.Delete.summary: Mark the current record for deletion.
CoreDb.Delete.remarks:
This function is like DBDelete().
VoDbDelete() and CoreDb.Delete() are aliases

CoreDb.Deleted.summary: Return the deleted status of the current record.
CoreDb.Deleted.returns: TRUE if the current record is marked for deletion; otherwise, FALSE. If there is no database file in use in the current work area, CoreDbDeleted() returns FALSE.

CoreDb.Eof.returns: TRUE when an attempt is made to move the record pointer beyond the last logical record in a database file or if the current database file contains no records; otherwise, FALSE. If there is no database file open in the current work area, CoreDbEOF() returns TRUE.
CoreDb.Eof.remarks: This function is like EOF(). VoDbEof() and CoreDb.Eof() are aliases

CoreDb.Eval.summary: Evaluate a code block for each record that matches a specified scope and/or condition.
CoreDb.Eval.param: The code block to execute for each record that matches the scope and conditions.
A code block that defines a condition that each record within the scope must meet in order to be processed.

A code block that defines another condition that each record must meet in order to be processed. As soon as a record is encountered that causes the condition to fail, the operation terminates. If no scope is specified, changes the default scope to .
The number of records to process, starting at the current record.
The number of the record to process.
TRUE processes only records from the current record to end-of-file. FALSE processes all records.
CoreDb.Eval.remarks: By default, Eval() operates on the currently selected work area. It will operate on an unselected work area if you specify it as part of an aliased expression. On each iteration, Eval() evaluates the specified code block. All records within the scope or matching the condition are processed until end-of-file is reached. Eval() can be used as a primitive for the construction of commands that process database files. In fact, many of the database processing commands are created using Eval().

CoreDb.FCount.summary: Return the number of fields in the current Workarea

CoreDb.FieldGet.summary: Retrieve the value of a specified database field.
CoreDb.FieldGet.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The returnvalue is returned through this parameter

CoreDb.FieldGetBytes.summary: Retrieve the value of a specified database field as an array of bytes
CoreDb.FieldGetBytes.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The returnvalue is returned through this parameter
CoreDb.FieldGetBytes.remarks: This function only works with RDDs that inherit from the DBF RDD. The Advantage RDD does not support this.

CoreDb.FieldInfo.param: The returnvalue is returned through this parameter. When set on entry this is the new value of the setting.

CoreDb.FieldName.summary: Return the name of a field as a string.
CoreDb.FieldName.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.

CoreDb.FieldPos.summary: Return the position of a field.

CoreDb.FieldPut.summary: Set the value of a specified database field.
CoreDb.FieldPut.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The value to write to the field

CoreDb.FieldPutBytes.summary: Retrieve the value of a specified database field as an array of bytes
CoreDb.FieldPutBytes.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The value to write to the field
CoreDb.FieldPutBytes.remarks: This function only works with RDDs that inherit from the DBF RDD. The Advantage RDD does not support this.

CoreDb.FileGet.summary: Import contents from file into Memo field
CoreDb.FileGet.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The name of the file where the value must be written.
CoreDb.FileGet.remarks:
This function is like DBFileGet().
VoDbFileGet() and CoreDb.FileGet() are aliases

CoreDb.FilePut.summary: Export field contents from Memo field to file
CoreDb.FilePut.param: The 1 based position of the field in the database file structure for the current work area.
The name of the file to write

CoreDb.Filter.remarks:
This function is like DBFilter().
VoDbFilter() and CoreDb.Filter() are aliases

CoreDb.Flock.summary: Lock an opened and shared database file.
CoreDb.Flock.remarks:
This function is like DBFlock().
VoDbFlock() and CoreDb.Flock() are aliases

CoreDb.Found.summary: Determine if the previous search operation succeeded.
CoreDb.Found.remarks:
This function is like Found().
VoDbFound() and CoreDb.Found() are aliases

CoreDb.GetScope.returns: An object containing the scope for the current area.

CoreDb.GetSelect.remarks:
This function is like DBGetSelect().
VoDbGetSelect() and CoreDb.GetSelect() are aliases

CoreDb.GoBottom.summary: Move to the last logical record.
CoreDb.GoBottom.remarks:
This function is like DBGoBottom().
VoDbGoBottom() and CoreDb.GoBottom() are aliases

CoreDb.GoTo.summary: Move to a record specified by record number.
CoreDb.GoTo.param: ID of the record to goto

CoreDb.GoToId.summary: Move to a record specified by record number.
CoreDb.GoToId.param: ID of the record to goto

CoreDb.GoTop.summary: Move to the first logical record.
CoreDb.GoTop.remarks:
This function is like DBGoTop().
VoDbGoTop() and CoreDb.GoTop() are aliases

CoreDb.Info.summary: Retrieve information about a work area.
CoreDb.Info.param: If specified, this parameter is used to change the value of a setting. This parameter also receives the return value.

CoreDb.JoinAppend.summary: Write values to destination Workarea in a JOIN operation

CoreDb.LastRec.summary: Return the number of the last record in a database file.

CoreDb.Locate.summary: Search for the first record that matches a specified condition and scope.
CoreDb.Locate.param: The number of records to process, starting with the current record.
The number of the record to process.
TRUE processes only records from the current record to end-of-file. FALSE processes all records.
CoreDb.Locate.remarks: This function is like DBLocate() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.Notify.summary: An event to which you can subscribe to be notified of operations on Workareas.

CoreDb.OrdBagExt.summary: Return the default index file extension for a work area as defined by the its RDD.
CoreDb.OrdBagExt.returns: Default extension for the current Workarea, or an empty string when no table is open in the current Workarea.

CoreDb.OrdCondSet.summary: Set the condition and scope for an order.
CoreDb.OrdCondSet.param: An object defining the condition and scope information.
CoreDb.OrdCondSet.remarks: This function is like OrdCondSet() but strongly typed and the condition information is passed in an object.

CoreDb.OrdCreate.summary: Create or replace an order in an index file.
CoreDb.OrdCreate.param: Orderbag name (index filename) to create the order in.
The order key expression specified as a string
The order key expression specified as a codeblock.
TRUE creates a unique order by including only those records with unique key values; FALSE uses all records in the database file.
An object defining the condition and scope information.
CoreDb.OrdCreate.remarks: This function is like DbCreateOrder() but strongly typed and the condition information is passed in an object.

CoreDb.OrdDestroy.summary: Remove an order from an open index file.
CoreDb.OrdDestroy.param: The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory. Use this argument with <cOrder> to remove ambiguity when there are two or more orders with the same name in different index files.

The name of the order or a number representing its position in the order list. Using the order name is the preferred method since the position may be difficult to determine using multiple-order index files. Invalid values are ignored.
CoreDb.OrdDestroy.remarks: This function is like DbDeleteOrder() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.OrderInfo.summary: Return information about index files and the orders in them.
CoreDb.OrderInfo.param: Specifies the type of information. This must match the values in the DBOI_ defines
The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory.
The name of the order about which you want to obtain information or a number representing its position in the order list.
If specified, this parameter is used to change the value of a setting.
If specified, this parameter is used to change the value of a setting and retrieve the current setting.
CoreDb.OrderInfo.remarks: This function is like DbOrderInfo() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.OrdListAdd.summary: Open an index file and add specified orders to the order list in a work area.
CoreDb.OrdListAdd.param: The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory. Use this argument with <cOrder> to remove ambiguity when there are two or more orders with the same name in different index files.

The name of the order or a number representing its position in the order list. Using the order name is the preferred method since the position may be difficult to determine using multiple-order index files. Invalid values are ignored.
CoreDb.OrdListAdd.remarks: This function is like DbSetIndex() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.OrdListClear.summary: Remove orders from the order list in a work area and close associated index files.
CoreDb.OrdListClear.param: The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory. Use this argument with <cOrder> to remove ambiguity when there are two or more orders with the same name in different index files.

The name of the order or a number representing its position in the order list. Using the order name is the preferred method since the position may be difficult to determine using multiple-order index files. Invalid values are ignored.
CoreDb.OrdListClear.remarks: This function is like DBClearIndex() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.OrdListRebuild.summary: Rebuild all orders in the order list of a work area.

CoreDb.OrdSetFocus.summary: Set the controlling order for a work area.
CoreDb.OrdSetFocus.param: The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory. Use this argument with <cOrder> to remove ambiguity when there are two or more orders with the same name in different index files.

The name of the order or a number representing its position in the order list. Using the order name is the preferred method since the position may be difficult to determine using multiple-order index files. Invalid values are ignored.
CoreDb.OrdSetFocus.remarks: This function is like DbSetOrder() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.OrdSetFocus_2.summary: Set the controlling order for a work area.
CoreDb.OrdSetFocus_2.param: The name of an index file, including an optional drive and directory. Use this argument with <cOrder> to remove ambiguity when there are two or more orders with the same name in different index files.

The name of the order or a number representing its position in the order list. Using the order name is the preferred method since the position may be difficult to determine using multiple-order index files. Invalid values are ignored.
This parameter returns the previous order
CoreDb.OrdSetFocus_2.remarks: This function is like DbSetOrder() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.Pack.summary: Remove all records that have been marked for deletion from a database file.
CoreDb.Pack.remarks:
This function is like DbPack().
VoDbPack() and CoreDb.Pack() are aliases

CoreDb.RddInfo.summary: Return and optionally change settings controlled directly by the RDD.
CoreDb.RddInfo.param: Ordinal number of the setting to set/retrieve.
The returnvalue is returned through this parameter. When set on entry this is the new value of the setting.

CoreDb.RddList.summary: Returns list of RDD names that are in use at this moment

CoreDb.RddSetDefault.summary: Return and optionally change the default RDD for the application.

CoreDb.Recall.summary: Restore the current record if it is marked for deletion.
CoreDb.Recall.remarks:
This function is like DBRecall().
VoDbRecall() and CoreDb.Recall() are aliases

CoreDb.RecordGet.summary: Get the contents of the current record as an array of bytes
CoreDb.RecordGet.returns: An array of bytes that contains the current record.

CoreDb.RecordInfo.param: This must match one of the values from the DbRecordInfo Enum
Some of the DbRecordInfo enum values require a record number
Some of the DbRecordInfo enum values require a new value.
The returnvalue is returned through this parameter. When set on entry this is the new value of the setting.

CoreDb.RecordPut.summary: Update the current record from an array of bytes
CoreDb.RecordPut.param: The bytes that form the record. Please note that if the DBF has a memo file, then this array must contain a valid position for the memo attached to the record.

CoreDb.RecSize.summary: Return the record length in the current Workarea

CoreDb.Refresh.summary: Discard all the changes to the current workares

CoreDb.Relation.summary: Return the linking expression of a specified relation.
CoreDb.Relation.param: The position of the desired relation in the list of current work area relations. The relations are numbered according to the order in which they were defined by relation setting.

CoreDb.RLock.param: The ID (usually a record number) of the record to be locked. If specified, record locks held by the current process are retained. If not specified, all locks held by the current process are released and the current record is assumed.
CoreDb.RLock.remarks: This function is like DBRlock() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.RSelect.summary: Return the work area number of a relation.
CoreDb.RSelect.param: The position of the desired relation in the list of work area relations. The relations are numbered according to the order in which they were defined by relation setting.
CoreDb.RSelect.remarks:
This function is like DBRSelect() but strongly typed.
VoDbRSelect() and CoreDb.RSelect() are aliases

CoreDb.Seek.summary: Move to the record having the specified key value.
CoreDb.Seek.param: Specifies the key value associated with the desired record.
Determines how the work area is positioned if the specified key value is not found: TRUE performs a soft seek; FALSE does not.
CoreDb.Seek.remarks: This function is like DBSeek() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.Select.summary: Select a new work area and retrieve the current work area.
CoreDb.Select.param: The work area number for the new work area.
The work area number for the old work area.
CoreDb.Select.remarks:
This function is like DBSelect() but strongly typed.
VoDbSelect() and CoreDb.Select() are aliases

CoreDb.SetFilter.param: Codeblock that defines the filter. Please note that some RDDs (such as Advangate) will NOT use this compiled codeblock.
String version of the filter. Some RDDs (such as Advantage) use this condition instead of the codeblock
CoreDb.SetFilter.remarks: This function is like DBSetFilter() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.SetFound.remarks: This function is like DBSetFound(). VoDbSetFound() and CoreDb.SetFound() are aliases

CoreDb.SetLocate.summary: Specify the code block for a locate condition.
CoreDb.SetLocate.remarks: This function is like DBSetLocate() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.SetRelation.summary: Relate a specified work area to the current work area.
CoreDb.SetRelation.param: Name of the relation. Defaults to the parent alias + "_" + Child alias
CoreDb.SetRelation.remarks: This function is like DBSetRelation() but strongly typed.

CoreDb.SetScope.param: A Scope objhect describing the current scope.

CoreDb.SetSelect.param: The number of the new work area. 0 indicates the first available free Workarea. -1 indicates the last available free Workarea.
CoreDb.SetSelect.remarks:
This function is like DBSetSelect() but strongly typed.
VoDbSetSelect() and CoreDb.SetSelect() are aliases

CoreDb.Skip.summary: Move the record pointer relative to the current record.
CoreDb.Skip.param: The number of logical records to move, relative to the current record. A positive value means to skip forward, and a negative value means to skip backward.
CoreDb.Skip.remarks:
This function is like DBSkip() but strongly typed.
DbSkip(), VoDbSkip() and CoreDb.Skip() are aliases

CoreDb.SkipScope.summary: Position the cursor relative to its current position within the current scope.

CoreDb.Sort.summary: Copy records to a database file in sorted order.
CoreDb.Sort.param: The work area number for the target Workarea.
The field names to write specified as an _FieldNames object.
The number of records to append, starting at the current record.
The number of the record to append.
TRUE processes only records from the current record to end-of-file. FALSE processes all records.
The sort keys, specified as an _FieldNames object.

CoreDb.SymSelect.summary: Select a new work area by specifying its alias as a string and return the number of the current work area.
CoreDb.SymSelect.param: The alias of the work area you want to select.

CoreDb.Trans.summary: Copy one or more rows from one work area to another.
CoreDb.Trans.param: The work area number for the target Workarea.
The field names to write specified as an _FieldNames object.
The sort keys, specified as an _FieldNames object.

CoreDb.TransRec.summary: Copy a single row from one work area to another.
CoreDb.TransRec.param: The work area number for the target Workarea.
The field names to write specified as an _FieldNames object.

CoreDb.Unlock.summary: Release all locks for a work area.
CoreDb.Unlock.param: The ID of the record to be unlocked. To omit, specify NULL_OBJECT. This unlocks all locked records or the whole file.

CoreDb.UnlockAll.summary: Release all locks for all work areas.
CoreDb.UnlockAll.remarks:
This function is like DBUnlockAll().
VoDbUnlockAll() and CoreDb.UnlockAll() are aliases

CoreDb.UseArea.param: RDDList structure that describes the RDD to use
Name of the (dbf) file to open
Alias to use for the Workarea
Should the file be opened shared ?
Should the file be opened readonly ?
CoreDb.UseArea.remarks:
This function is like DbUseArea() but strongly typed.
VoDbUseArea() and CoreDb.UseArea() are aliases

CoreDb.UseArea_2.param: Name of the RDD to use.

CoreDb.UseArea_3.param: Type of the RDD to use.

CoreDb.Zap.summary: Remove all records from the current Workarea.
CoreDb.Zap.remarks:
This function is like DBZap().
VoDbZap() and CoreDb.Zap() are aliases

CToDt.summary: Convert a Date string to DateTime.
CToDt.param: A string of numbers representing the month, day, and year, separated by any character other than a number. The month, day, and year digits must be in the format set by SetDateFormat() or SetDateCountry(). If the century digits are not specified, the century is determined by the rules of SetEpoch().
A string representating the date format to use when converting the string to a date. Should consist of D, M and Y characters and separators.
CToDt.returns: The DateTime value that corresponds to the numbers specified in . If is not a valid date, CToDt() returns a DateTime.MinValue.

CToDtAnsi.summary: Convert an ANSI date string to DateTime
CToDtAnsi.param: A string in the ANSI form yyyy.mm.dd, where yy, mm, and dd represent year, month, and day respectively. The year, month, and day can be separated by any character other than a number. cDate is always interpreted as an ANSI string and is not dependent on SetDateFormat() or SetDateCountry(). If the century digits are not specified, the century is determined by the rules of SetEpoch().
CToDtAnsi.returns: The date value that corresponds to the numbers specified in . If cDate is not a valid ANSI date, CToDAnsi() returns a DateTime.MinValue.

CTOL.summary: Convert a string value to a logic.

CursorProperty.summary: Enum that matches the various FoxPro cursor properties, used in CursorGetProp() and CursorSetProp()

DatabasePropertyCollection.Add.summary: Add a property - value pair to the collection.

DatabasePropertyCollection.GetValue.summary: Get the value for a property
DatabasePropertyCollection.GetValue.returns: The value from the collection or an empty value of the right type.

DatabasePropertyCollection.GetValue_T_.summary: Get the value for a property or an empty value when the property has not been defined
DatabasePropertyCollection.GetValue_T_.typeparam: Expected return type for the value
DatabasePropertyCollection.GetValue_T_.returns: The value from the collection or an empty value of the right type.

DatabasePropertyType.summary: Enum that matches the various FoxPro database properties, used in DbGetProp() and DbSetProp()

DatabasePropertyType.AllowSimultaneousFe.summary: Applies when using remote views, a shared connection, and to cursors created using ODBC.

DatabasePropertyType.Asynchronous.summary: The connection mode. (Default) False (.F.) specifies a synchronous connection. True (.T.) specifies an asynchronous connection.Read/write.

DatabasePropertyType.BatchMode.summary: The batch-processing mode. (Default) True (.T.) specifies the connection that operates in batch mode.

DatabasePropertyType.BatchUpdateCount.summary: The number of update statements sent to the back end for views. 1 is the default. Adjusting this value can greatly increase update performance. (N)

DatabasePropertyType.Caption.summary: The field caption. (C) If an equal sign (=) precedes the value of this property, Visual FoxPro evaluates the value as an expression. Otherwise, it is treated as a string literal.Read/write. FIELD

DatabasePropertyType.ColumnName.summary: X# Column Name property (not persisted in DBC)

DatabasePropertyType.Comment.summary: The text of the field comment. (C)

DatabasePropertyType.CompareMemo.summary: Contains true (.T.) (default) if memo fields (of type Memo, General, or Picture, or, for remote views, type Timestamp) are included in the WHERE clause for updates; otherwise, contains false (.F.).Read/write.

DatabasePropertyType.ConnectName.summary: The named connection used when the view is opened. (C)

DatabasePropertyType.ConnectTimeout.summary: The connection timeout interval in seconds. The default is 0 (wait indefinitely).

DatabasePropertyType.Database.summary: The name of the server database specified with the DATABASE clause in the CREATE CONNECTION command or in the Connection Designer.

DatabasePropertyType.DataSource.summary: The name of the data source as defined in the Odbc.ini file.

DatabasePropertyType.DataType.summary: The data type for a field in a view. Initially set to the data type for the field in the data source.

DatabasePropertyType.DBCEventFileName.summary: Relative path and filename of external program file containing DBC Events code. (C)

DatabasePropertyType.DBCEvents.summary: Enabled state of DBC Events. Set to True (.T.) to enable. (L)

DatabasePropertyType.DisconnectRollback.summary: Specifies if a pending transaction is committed or rolled back when SQLDISCONNECT( ) is called for the last connection handle.

DatabasePropertyType.DisplayClass.summary: Name of the class used for field mapping. (C)

DatabasePropertyType.DisplayClassLibrary.summary: Path to the class library specified with the DisplayClass property.(C)

DatabasePropertyType.DispLogin.summary: Contains a numeric value that determines when the ODBC Login dialog box is displayed.

DatabasePropertyType.DispWarnings.summary: Contains a numeric value that determines when the ODBC Login dialog box is displayed.

DatabasePropertyType.FetchAsNeeded.summary: If True (.T.), data is fetched only when needed, such as when record pointer moves to a row that has not been fetched. If False (.F.), additional data is fetched during idle time. (Default)

DatabasePropertyType.FetchMemo.summary: Contains true (.T.) (default) if memo and general fields are fetched with the view results; otherwise, contains false (.F.).

DatabasePropertyType.FetchSize.summary: Contains the number of records fetched at a time from the remote tables. The default is 100 records. Setting FetchSize to –1 retrieves the complete result set, limited by the MaxRecords setting

DatabasePropertyType.IdleTimeout.summary: The idle timeout interval in minutes. Timeout is checked no more than once per minute, therefore it is possible that active connections might deactivate one or two minutes later after the specified time interval has elapsed. The default value is 0 (wait indefinitely).

DatabasePropertyType.IsUnique.summary: The IsUnique flag for an Index Object

DatabasePropertyType.KeyField.summary: Contains True (.T.) if the field is specified in an index key expression; otherwise, contains False (.F.). (L)

DatabasePropertyType.MaxRecords.summary: The maximum number of records fetched when result sets are returned. (N) The default is – 1 (all rows are returned). A value of 0 specifies that the view is executed but no results are fetched.

DatabasePropertyType.OffLine.summary: Is the table offline (this is not stored as a real property but derived from the extistence of the properties OfflineRecs, OfflineRemRecs and Path.

DatabasePropertyType.OfflineRecs.summary: When this property is found the table is offline. The Path property then returns the OffLinePath

DatabasePropertyType.PacketSize.summary: The size of the network packet used by the connection. Adjusting this value can improve performance. The default value is 4096 bytes (4K).

DatabasePropertyType.ParameterList.summary: The WHERE clause parameters. The format for the parameters is ''ParameterName1, 'Type1'; ParameterName2, 'Type2'; ...''

DatabasePropertyType.Path.summary: The relative path with respect to the DBC to the table including the name of the file.

DatabasePropertyType.Prepared.summary: Contains True (.T.) if SQL statements are prepared for subsequent REQUERY( ) function calls. REQUERY( ) is used to retrieve data again for a SQL view. See SQLPREPARE( ) for additional information about preparing SQL statements. The default is false (.F.).

DatabasePropertyType.PrimaryKey.summary: The tag name of the primary key. (C)

DatabasePropertyType.QueryTimeOut.summary: The query timeout interval in seconds. The default value is 0 (wait indefinitely).

DatabasePropertyType.RelatedTable.summary: Related table for a Relation Object

DatabasePropertyType.RelatedTag.summary: Related tag for a Relatin Object

DatabasePropertyType.SendUpdates.summary: Contains true (.T.) if a SQL update query is sent to update remote tables; otherwise, contains false (.F.) (default).

DatabasePropertyType.ShareConnection.summary: Contains true (.T.) if the view can share its connection handle with other connections; otherwise, contains false (.F.).

DatabasePropertyType.SourceType.summary: The view source. (N) SourceType may assume the following values: 1 - The view uses local tables.2 - The view uses remote tables.

DatabasePropertyType.SQL.summary: The SQL statement executed when the view is opened.(C)

DatabasePropertyType.Tables.summary: A comma-delimited list of the names of the tables.(C)

DatabasePropertyType.Transactions.summary: Contains a numeric value that determines how the connection manages transactions on the remote table.

DatabasePropertyType.Updatable.summary: Contains True (.T.) if the field can be updated; otherwise, contains False (.F.).

DatabasePropertyType.UpdateName.summary: The name of the field used when data in the field is updated to the remote table. By default, the remote table field name.Read/write.

DatabasePropertyType.UpdateType.summary: The update type. Valid values are: 1 or DB_UPDATE (from Foxpro.h). The old data is updated with the new data (default). 2 or DB_DELETEINSERT (from Foxpro.h). The old data is deleted and the new data is inserted.

DatabasePropertyType.UseMemoSize.summary: The minimum size (in bytes) for which result columns are returned in memo fields. For example, if the width of a column result is greater than the value of UseMemoSize, the column result is stored in a memo field. UseMemoSize may vary from 1 to 255; the default value is 255.

DatabasePropertyType.WaitTime.summary: The amount of time in milliseconds that elapses before Visual FoxPro checks whether the SQL statement has completed executing.

DatabasePropertyType.WhereType.summary: The WHERE clause for updates to remote tables.WhereType may assume the following values: 1 or DB_KEY , 2 or DB_KEYANDUPDATABLE, 3 or DB_KEYANDMODIFIED and 4 or DB_KEYANDTIMESTAMP

DataSession.Add.summary: Add a DataSession to the list of open datasessions

DataSession.Close.summary: Remove a DataSession from the list of open datasessions

DataSession.Id.summary: The unique id for the datasession

DateCountry.summary: Values that match the XBase DateCountry defines. They are used to set the date format.
DateCountry.remarks: Please note that although there are different values for British and French, the date formats are the same.

Dbc.summary: Helper class to open, close and select a database

Dbc.Dump.summary: Dump a database to the terminal window.

Dbc.DumpProperties.summary: Dump the conents of a properties collection.

Dbc.IsUsed.summary: Check if a database is used/opened.

DbcConnection.summary: Class that stores information about an ODBC connection in a DBC

DbcDatabase.Area.summary: Area number for the cursor in the Databases Datasession

DbcDatabase.FindConnection.summary: Find a connection in the Database container.
DbcDatabase.FindConnection.param: The connection name to look for.

DbcDatabase.FindTable.summary: Find a table in the Database container.
DbcDatabase.FindTable.param: The table name to look for.

DbcDatabase.FindView.summary: Find a view in the Database container.
DbcDatabase.FindView.param: The view name to look for.

DbcDatabase.GetData.summary: Loads the tables, views and connections from the DBC file

DbcField.summary: Class that stores information about a field in a DBC

DbcIndex.summary: Class that stores information about an index in a DBC

DbcManager.summary: This classes manages open database. Both databases that are opened explicitely and also databases that are opened for "free" tables that have a backlink to a database
DbcManager.remarks:
The Databases are opened like 'normal' tables but in a separate DataSession.
The DoForDataBase() method selects this special datasession, executes the action that is passed as parameter and restores the original datasession. This way you can seek, evaluate macros etc without disturbing the normal opened cursors.

DbcManager.Close.param: Alias name of the database to close.
DbcManager.Close.returns: TRUE when the Database was open and was closed succesfully, otherwise FALSE.

DbcManager.CreateDatabase.param: The fully qualified filename to create.
DbcManager.CreateDatabase.returns: TRUE when the database file was created successfully.

DbcManager.ExtendDbName.summary: Enhance the DBC name. Adds the extension and path when needed.
DbcManager.ExtendDbName.param: Name of the file. The extension and path are optional.
DbcManager.ExtendDbName.returns: Name with DBC extension when needed and with full path information when found.

DbcManager.FindDatabase.summary: Search a Database by file name
DbcManager.FindDatabase.param: The fully qualified filename to look for, so MUST have an extension and path.
DbcManager.FindDatabase.returns: The database object (when found) otherwise NULL_OBJECT.

DbcManager.FindDatabaseByName.param: Alias name of the database to close.
DbcManager.FindDatabaseByName.returns: The database object (when found) otherwise NULL_OBJECT.

DbcManager.Open.summary: Open a DBC file. This does NOT make the database the active database
DbcManager.Open.param: Filename of the database to open.
Should the database be opened in Shared mode
Should the database be opened Readonly
Should the structure of the database be validated.
DbcManager.Open.returns: TRUE when opened succesfully. When the database was already open then FALSE is returned.

DbcManager.ValidateStructure.returns: TRUE when the structure is OK, otherwise FALSE.

DbColumnInfo.CalculateColumnType.summary: Calculate the column type from the FieldType

DbColumnInfo.ctor.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DbColumnInfo class

DbColumnInfo.ctor_2.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DbColumnInfo class

DbcOther.summary: Class that stores other info about Databases, such as the source and object code for Database

DbcRelation.summary: Class that stores information about a relation in a DBC

DbcRelation.RIInfo.summary: The relational rules for Insert, Update, Delete.

DbcTable.summary: Class that stores information about a table in a DBC
DbcTable.remarks: When opened the Fields, Indexes and Relations are not read. They need to be explicitely loaded with a call to GetData()

DbcTable.Database.summary: The database in which this table is defined.

DbcTable.Path.summary: The Table path as stored in the DBC file.

DbCurrency.summary: Implementation of the ICurrency interface that can be used by the RDD system.

DbcView.summary: Class that stores information about a view in a DBC
DbcView.remarks: When opened the Fields etc are not read. They need to be explicitely loaded with a call to GetData()

DbcView.Database.summary: The database in which this table is defined.

DbDate.summary: Implementation of the IDate interface that can be used by the RDD system.

DbEvalInfo.summary: Helper class to store the scope and codeblock for a DbEval() operation.

DbEvalInfo.Block.summary: A code block to be evaluated with DbEval() on each row of the work area that is in the range defined by ScopeInfo.

DbEvalInfo.ScopeInfo.summary: A DbScopeInfo structure limiting the evaluation of Block.

DbFilterInfo.summary: Helper class to store a filter condition for a table.

DbFilterInfo.Active.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a filter is active.

DbFilterInfo.FilterBlock.summary: A code block representing the condition that is evaluated at each cursor location. If the result of the evaluation is FALSE, the cursor location requested is invalid according to the current filter condition.

DbFilterInfo.FilterText.summary: A string representing the source code for itmCobExpr.

DbFilterInfo.Optimized.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a filter is optimized.

DbFloat.summary: Implementation of the IFloat interface that can be used by the RDD system.

DbLockInfo.summary: Helper structure to store information needed to lock a row or table for exclusive access.

DbLockInfo.Method.summary: A constant indicating the type of lock to obtain. The possible values are of the Lockmethod enum.

DbLockInfo.RecId.summary: An Item indicating the ID of the row to lock. This member is meaningful only if Method is set to EXCLUSIVE or MULTIPLE.

DbLockInfo.Result.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the lock operation was successful.

DbNotifyEventArgs.summary: Provides data for the Notify event of the IDbNotify interface.

DbNotifyEventArgs.Data.summary: Specific data that describes the object.

DbNotifyEventArgs.Type.summary: The type of event that happened.

DbOpenInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed to open a table.

DbOpenInfo.Alias.summary: Unique Alias that the table has. This must be a valid XBase Identifier

DbOpenInfo.Extension.summary: The extension for the table that must be opened.

DbOpenInfo.FileMode.summary: Return the numeric FileMode based on the Shared and Readonly flags

DbOpenInfo.FileName.summary: The filename (optionally includig a path) for the table that must be opened. Does not have an extension.

DbOpenInfo.FullName.summary: The full name (filename + extension) of the file.

DbOpenInfo.Shared.summary: Should the table be opened Shared?

DbOpenInfo.Workarea.summary: Workarea number in which the table will be opened.

DbOrder_Info.DBOI_STRICTREAD.summary: The following numbers are reserved but not implemented yet.

DbOrderCondInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed to create a conditional order.

DbOrderCondInfo.Active.summary: A flag that is TRUE if one or more valid conditions have been specified in the structure.

DbOrderCondInfo.Additive.summary: A flag that is TRUE if open orders should remain open while the new order is being created.

DbOrderCondInfo.All.summary: A flag that is TRUE if all rows are to be processed during order creation.

DbOrderCondInfo.Collation.summary: Collation for the FoxPro INDEX ON command

DbOrderCondInfo.Custom.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the new order will be a custom built order.

DbOrderCondInfo.Descending.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the order should be created in descending order.

DbOrderCondInfo.EvalBlock.summary: A code block defining the expression to evaluate every StepSize rows during the creation of the order. The code block referenced should return a logical value: TRUE indicates that creation of the order should continue normally, and FALSE indicates that order creation should terminate.

DbOrderCondInfo.ForBlock.summary: A code block defining the for condition to use for the creation and maintenance of the order.

DbOrderCondInfo.ForExpression.summary: A string defining the for condition to use for the creation and maintenance of the order.

DbOrderCondInfo.IsBinary.summary: Binary option for the FoxPro INDEX ON command

DbOrderCondInfo.IsCandidate.summary: Candidate option for the FoxPro INDEX ON command

DbOrderCondInfo.NextCount.summary: The number of rows to process for order creation.

DbOrderCondInfo.NoOptimize.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the for condition may NOT be optimized,

DbOrderCondInfo.RecNo.summary: A single row number to include in the order.

DbOrderCondInfo.Rest.summary: A flag that is TRUE if only the rows specified by lStartRecno through end-of-file are to be included in the order.

DbOrderCondInfo.Scoped.summary: A flag indicating whether the order is to be scoped. fScoped will be TRUE if WhileBlock, NextCount, Recno, Rest, or All is specified.

DbOrderCondInfo.StartRecNo.summary: The row at which to begin processing when either the NextCount or Rest scoping options are specified.

DbOrderCondInfo.StepSize.summary: The frequency of the evaluation of EvalBlock.

DbOrderCondInfo.UseCurrent.summary: A flag that is TRUE if only rows in the controlling order are to be included in the order.

DbOrderCondInfo.WhileBlock.summary: A code block defining the while condition to use for the creation of the order. An empty value indicates that no while condition is being imposed.

DbOrderCondInfo.WhileExpression.summary: A string defining the for while condition to use for the creation and maintenance of the order.

DbOrderCreateInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed to create a new order.

DbOrderCreateInfo.Block.summary: A code block containing the key expression defining the order imposed on the work area.

DbOrderCreateInfo.Expression.summary: The key expression defining the order.

DbOrderCreateInfo.OrdCondInfo.summary: A DbOrderCondInfo object containing information about the condition (if any) for the order.

DbOrderCreateInfo.Order.summary: The order name or number to create in BagName.

DbOrderCreateInfo.Unique.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the order should contain only unique keys.

DbOrderInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed to open/address an order.

DbOrderInfo.AllTags.summary: A flag that is TRUE if all tags of the index file must be opened.

DbOrderInfo.Expression.summary: A code block containing the key expression defining the order imposed on the work area.

DbOrderInfo.Order.summary: An object containing the order name or number

DbOrderInfo.Result.summary: Return value for some order operations.

DbRegisterClient.summary: Register an object that will receive notification messages for Workarea events.
DbRegisterClient.returns: TRUE when registration succceeded. FALSE when the client was already registered

DbRelInfo.summary: Helper class to store a list of relational information.

DbRelInfo.Block.summary: A code block used to reposition the cursor of the child table when this relation is resolved.

DbRelInfo.Child.summary: A reference to the child RDD for the relation.

DbRelInfo.Key.summary: The expression used to reposition the cursor of the child table when this relation is resolved.

DbRelInfo.Name.summary: name of the relation. Defaults to the parent alias = '_' + child alias.

DbRelInfo.Parent.summary: A reference to the parent RDD for the relation.

DbScopeInfo.summary: Helper class to store references to all of the scope clause expressions.

DbScopeInfo.ForBlock.summary: A code block representing the conditional for clause. A for condition is, essentially, a filter that hides rows for which the condition evaluates to FALSE. The string value is provided for storage, while the code block is provided as a parameter for the EvalBlock() method.

DbScopeInfo.ForExpression.summary: A string representing the conditional for clause. A for condition is, essentially, a filter that hides rows for which the condition evaluates to FALSE. The string value is provided for storage, while the code block is provided as a parameter for the EvalBlock() method.

DbScopeInfo.IgnoreDuplicates.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a process should ignore duplicate key values.

DbScopeInfo.IgnoreFilter.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a process should ignore any filter condition imposed on the current work area.

DbScopeInfo.IncludeDeleted.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a process should include deleted rows.

DbScopeInfo.Last.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the last row of the current scope is required.

DbScopeInfo.NextCount.summary: Permits continuation of a process for the next lNext rows, while obeying for and while clauses.

DbScopeInfo.RecId.summary: Permits continuation of a process for a single row number, while obeying for and while clauses.

DbScopeInfo.Rest.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a process should continue stepping through data from the current work area cursor position until logical end-of-file.

DbScopeInfo.WhileBlock.summary: A code block representing the conditional while clause. A while condition permits continuation of a process that steps through rows until the condition evaluates to FALSE. The string value is provided for storage, while the code block is provided as a parameter for the EvalBlock() method.

DbScopeInfo.WhileExpression.summary: A string representing the conditional while clause. A while condition permits continuation of a process that steps through rows until the condition evaluates to FALSE. The string value is provided for storage, while the code block is provided as a parameter for the EvalBlock() method.

DbSeekInfo.summary: Helper structure to store information needed to perform a seek operation

DbSeekInfo.Last.summary: A flag that is TRUE if the last occurrence of the specified key value is to be sought, rather than the first.

DbSeekInfo.SoftSeek.summary: A flag that is TRUE if a soft seek is to be performed.

DbSeekInfo.Value.summary: An object containing the key value to find.

DbSortInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed to perform a physical sort.

DbSortInfo.ItemCount.summary: Number of items in the Items array.

DbSortInfo.Items.summary: An array of DbSortItem structures defining the key values for the sort. Note that the key values are processed in the order that they appear in this array.

DbSortInfo.TransInfo.summary: A DbTransInfo object holding the destination work area, column transfer information, and scoping information for the Sort() method.

DbSortItem.summary: Helper structure to store information about a single sort key value.

DbSortItem.FieldNo.summary: A one-based index indicating the column on which the sort is based.

DbSortItem.Flags.summary: One or more constants that function as sort optimization and control flags. They are passed to your RDD Sort() routine from the high-level wrapper function for the DBSort() function.

DbSortItem.Length.summary: The length of the field in the Workarea buffer.

DbSortItem.OffSet.summary: The offset of the field in the Workarea buffer.

DbTransInfo.summary: Helper class to store information needed for the global transfer of data items from one work area to another.

DbTransInfo.Flags.summary: Transfer attributes specified using one or more of the constants Match or PutRec.

DbTransInfo.ItemCount.summary: Number of items in the Items array.

DbTransInfo.Items.summary: An array of DbTransItem structures defining the items to transfer to the destination work area. This is usually a list of column mappings from the source to the destination.

DbTransInfo.Scope.summary: A DbScopeInfo object describing the limits of the scope of the transfer.

DbTransItem.summary: Helper structure to store information about a single piece of data (usually a column) to transfer from one work area to another.

DbTransItem.Destination.summary: A one-based field index in the destination work area.

DbTransItem.Source.summary: A one-based field index in the source work area.

DbUnRegisterClient.summary: Unregister an object that receives notification messages for Workarea events.
DbUnRegisterClient.returns: TRUE when unregistration succceeded. FALSE when the the client was not registered.

DefaultParameterValueAttribute.summary: this class is used to mark default parameter values in the middle of a parameter list.
DefaultParameterValueAttribute.remarks:
It is used when compiling in VO or Vulcan mode. And only for parameters that are followed by parameters without default value (because that is not supported by the .Net framework).
The X# compiler will find this attribute at compile time and will insert its value into the argument list.

DefaultParameterValueAttribute.Flag.summary: Flag indicating the type of the default parameter

item.description: Date (value is LONG ticks, empty for NULL_DATE)
Symbol, value can be empty or a string
PSZ, null = empty, or a string

DtToC.summary: Convert a DateTime to a string.
DtToC.returns: A string representation of the given Date, formatted in the current Date format.

DtToS.summary: Convert a DateTime value to a string formatted as string in ANSI format
DtToS.returns: An 8-character string in the format yyyymmdd. If dDate is a DateTime.MinValue, a string of eight spaces is returned. The return value is not affected by the current date format.

ErrorStack.summary: Return the error stack as a string.
ErrorStack.returns: The error stack with line numbers. In the VO and Vulcan dialect the stack is in "VO Format"

ErrorStack_2.summary: Return the error stack as a string.
ErrorStack_2.param: StackTrace object to convert to an error stack string
ErrorStack_2.returns: The error stack with line numbers. In the VO and Vulcan dialect the stack is in "VO Format"

FError.summary: Set the error code for a file operation.
FError.returns: The previous errorcode from the last file operation.

FError_2.summary: Get the error code for a file operation.
FError_2.returns: The error from the last file operation or the last user-specified setting. If there was no error, FError() returns 0.

FException.summary: Get the last exception for a file operation.
FException.returns: The exception from the last file operation or the last user-specified setting. If there was no exception, FException() returns null.

FGetBuffer.summary: Access or allocate the File I/O Buffer used by the X# Runtime for Low Level File Access for a file.
FGetBuffer.param: The size of the buffer that you would like to allocate.s
FGetBuffer.returns: The Byte[] associated with the file handle for an open file. When the file handle is invalid then a NULL object will be returned.
FGetBuffer.remarks:
This function will either return the existing buffer or allocate a new buffer of the requested size.
When there is already a buffer allocated for the file handle, and this buffer is large enough then the existing buffer is returned. When the size requested exceeds the size of the allocated buffer, or when no buffer exists, then a new byte array will be allocated.

FGetFileName.summary: Return the filename for a file handle

FGetStream.summary: Access the FileStream object used by the X# Runtime for Low Level File Access
FGetStream.returns: The FileStream object or NULL when the handle is not valid

remarks.note:
You are not supposed to close the stream object that you retrieve with this function. The Lifetime management of the stream should be left to the X# Runtime
If you want to close the stream, please use the FClose() function

FileStreamExensions.SafeLock.summary: Locks a region in a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism.
FileStreamExensions.SafeLock.param: Start of the region to lock. The method checks to see that the location is >= 0.
Size of the region to lock. The method checks to see that the size is > 0.
FileStreamExensions.SafeLock.returns: TRUE when succesfull, FALSE when an exception occurred during reading or when offset or length were invalid.

FileStreamExensions.SafeRead.summary: Read data from a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism. Reads from current location.

FileStreamExensions.SafeRead_2.summary: Read data from a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism. Reads from current location.

FileStreamExensions.SafeReadAt.summary: Read data at a location in the file. Makes sure that file locations are >= 0

FileStreamExensions.SafeSetLength.summary: Sets the length of a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism.
FileStreamExensions.SafeSetLength.param: The stream for which to set the lengt.
The new length of the stream. The method checks to see that the location is >= 0.
FileStreamExensions.SafeSetLength.returns: TRUE when succesfull, FALSE when an exception occurred during reading or when the length < 0

FileStreamExensions.SafeSetPos.summary: Sets the location of a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism. .

FileStreamExensions.SafeUnlock.summary: Unlocks a region in a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism.
FileStreamExensions.SafeUnlock.param: Start of the region to unlock. The method checks to see that the location is >= 0.
Size of the region to unlock. The method checks to see that the size is > 0.
FileStreamExensions.SafeUnlock.returns: TRUE when succesfull, FALSE when an exception occurred during reading or when offset or length were invalid.

FileStreamExensions.SafeWrite.summary: Write data to a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism. Writes to the current location.

FileStreamExensions.SafeWriteAt.summary: Write data at a location in the file. Makes sure that file locations are >= 0.

FileStreamExensions.SafeWriteByte.summary: Write data to a stream. Contains TRY CATCH mechanism. Writes to the current location.

FRead3.param: An array of bytes to store the data read from the specified file. The length of this variable must be greater than or equal to the number of bytes in the next parameter.

FRead4.param: An array of bytes to store the data read from the specified file. The length of this variable must be greater than or equal to the number of bytes in the next parameter.

FSeek3.remarks:
The possible values for the origin are:
Constant Seeks from
FS_END End-of-file
FS_RELATIVE Current pointer position
FS_SET Beginning-of-file

FSize.summary: Returns the size in bytes of a specified file.
FSize.returns: The size of the file or -1 when the file handle is not valid.

FSize_2.summary: Returns the size in bytes of a specified file.
FSize_2.param: Specifies a file for which FSIZE( ) returns the size in bytes.
FSize_2.returns: The size of the file or -1 when the file is not found.

overloads.summary: Write a string to an open file

FWrite.param: If FALSE an OEM to ANSI conversion is made.

overloads.summary: Write a string, a carriage-return character, and a linefeed character to an open file.

Gencode.summary: Error code Enum that matches the Visual Objecs and Vulcan Generic Error Codes
Gencode.note: These enums are also available as DEFINES and can therefore also be used without the "Gencode." prefix.

GetChunkBase64.summary: This function is not implemented yet

GetMacroCompiler.summary: Get the type of the class that is used to compile macros
GetMacroCompiler.returns: The type of the currently defined MacroCompiler. This may be NULL if no type has been set yet and no macros have been compiled.

GetMimType.summary: This function is not implemented yet

GetTimeZoneDiff.summary: Returns TimeZone difference for current timezone in Hours

IBlobData.summary: Interface that is used for BLOB operations for memo files

IBlobData.Data.summary: Data to write to the blob file. This can be a BYTE[] but also other values. At this moment the FPT RDD supports BYTE[], String, Logic Other value types may follow later

IBlobData.Length.summary: Length of the data to write or return. Defaults to all data

IBlobData.Pointer.summary: Pointer for which to write or return the data

IBlobData.Start.summary: Start of the data to write or return. Defaults to 0

ICodeblock.summary: This interface defines Compile time and runtime codeblocks

ICodeblock.PCount.summary: Returns the number of parameters defined for the codeblock

ICodeblock2.summary: This interface extends the ICodeblock interface and adds a property that returns the 'real' returntype of the codeblock, before customizations are done to the value (for example for Foxpro where NIL gets converted to FALSE)

ICodeblock2.ResultType.summary: Returns the real /original return type of the EvalBlock() method

ICurrency.summary: This interface is used to return Decimal values from the RDD system

IDate.Day.summary: Day part of the date. A number between 0 an 31

IDate.Month.summary: Month part of the date. A number between 0 an 12

IDate.Year.summary: Year part of the date. A number between 0 and 9999

IDbNotify.summary: This interface must be implemented by objects that register themselves for DB Notifications

IDbRow.summary: This interface defines a row in a .Net Table.

IFloat.Digits.summary: Number of digits (includes the optional decimal separator and decimals).

IMacroCompiler.summary: This interface defines the Macro compiler subsystem

IMacroCompiler.Compile.summary: Compile a string into a runtime codeblock.
IMacroCompiler.Compile.param: will be set to TRUE when the string was a real codeblock (with {|..| }).
will be set to TRUE when the macro contains code that may result in adding new MemVars).

IMacroCompiler2.summary: This interface extends the Macro compiler and adds a method that is called to decide between ambigous methods or constructors

IMacroCompiler2.Resolver.summary: This property allows to set a delegate that may be used to resolve ambigous references

IMemo.summary: This interface defines the mimimum methods that a RDD that supports memos should implement.

IMemo.CreateMemFile.param: object describing the file to create.

IMemo.Flush.summary: Flush the changes to the memo file

IMemo.GetValue.summary: Get a value from a memo file for the specified column.
IMemo.GetValue.param: 1 based column number for which the value should be retrieved.

IMemo.GetValueFile.summary: Get the value for a column and write (export) it to an external file.
IMemo.GetValueFile.param: 1 based column number for which the value should be retrieved.
Name of the file that needs to be written to.

IMemo.GetValueLength.summary: Get the length of the for the specified column.
IMemo.GetValueLength.param: 1 based column number for which the length should be retrieved.

IMemo.OpenMemFile.param: object describing the file to open.

IMemo.PutValue.summary: Write a value to a memo file for a specified column
IMemo.PutValue.param: 1 based column number for which the value should be written.
New value that needs to written to the memo file for this column.

IMemo.PutValueFile.summary: Read (Import) a value from an external file and write it to the specified column.
IMemo.PutValueFile.param: 1 based column number for which the value should be written.
Name of the file that needs to be read from.

IMemo.Zap.summary: Physically remove all memos from the memofile.

IOrder.summary: This interface defines the mimimum methods that a RDD that supports indexes should implement.

IOrder.Flush.summary: Flush the changes to the index.

IOrder.Found.summary: Result of the last seek operation for the current Workarea.

IOrder.OrderCondition.summary: Set the condition for the next Index Creation
IOrder.OrderCondition.param: An object containing information for the order condition.

IOrder.OrderCreate.summary: Create a new index or tag.
IOrder.OrderCreate.param: An object containing information for order creation.

IOrder.OrderDestroy.param: An object containing information about the order to remove.

IOrder.OrderInfo.summary: Retrieve information about the current index.

IOrder.OrderListAdd.summary: Open an index file and add to the list of open indexes for the current Workarea.
IOrder.OrderListAdd.param: An object containing information about the orderlist (file) to add.

IOrder.OrderListDelete.summary: Close an index file and remove it from the list of open indexes for the current Workarea.
IOrder.OrderListDelete.param: An object containing information about the orderlist (file) to delete.

IOrder.OrderListFocus.summary: Set focus to another index in the list open indexes for the current Workarea.
IOrder.OrderListFocus.param: An object containing information about the order to select.

IOrder.OrderListRebuild.summary: Rebuild all indexes for the current Workarea.

IOrder.Seek.summary: Perform a seek operation on the current selected index for the current Workarea.
IOrder.Seek.param: An object containing containing the necessary seek information.

IRawData.summary: An RDD that implements this interface will return the data for memo fields as BYTE[] when the ReturnRawData property is set TRUE

IRdd.summary: Definition for the interface that each RDD must implement

IRdd.Append.summary: Append a blank row and position the cursor to the new row.
IRdd.Append.param: A flag that is TRUE if you want to clear all pending row locks before appending the new row and FALSE if you want to add the new row to the end of the current lock list.

IRdd.AppendLock.summary: Add a newly appended row to the list of locked rows.
IRdd.AppendLock.param: An enum value specifying the kind of lock to acquire.

IRdd.BlobInfo.summary: Retrieve information about a memo column.

IRdd.BoF.summary: Is the table at the logical beginning-of-file.

IRdd.ChildEnd.summary: Report the initialization of a relation.
IRdd.ChildEnd.param: An object containing information about the relation

IRdd.ChildStart.summary: Report the initialization of a relation.
IRdd.ChildStart.param: An object containing information about the relation

IRdd.ChildSync.summary: Post a pending relational movement, indicating that the specified child work area has been affected by a parental movement.
IRdd.ChildSync.param: An object containing information about the relation

IRdd.Continue.summary: Goto the next record based on the corrent locate condition.

IRdd.Create.param: object describing the file to create.

IRdd.CreateFields.summary: Add columns defined in an array.

IRdd.CreateMemFile.param: object describing the file to create.

IRdd.DbEval.summary: Evaluate a code block for each row.
IRdd.DbEval.param: object containing the information about which rows to visit.

IRdd.Delete.summary: Mark the row at the current cursor position for deletion.

IRdd.EoF.summary: Is the table at the logical end-of-file.

IRdd.FieldCount.summary: The # of fields in the current Workarea.

IRdd.FieldIndex.summary: Return the ONE based field index for a field name.
IRdd.FieldIndex.param: The name of the field in the database file structure. When the name is not found it will also check for a field alias with this name.
IRdd.FieldIndex.returns: The ONE based position of the field in the database file structure.

IRdd.FieldInfo.summary: Retrieve and optionally change information about a column.
IRdd.FieldInfo.param: The ONE based position of the field in the database file structure.
If specified (not null), then this parameter is used to change the value of a setting.
IRdd.FieldInfo.returns: The current value for the property indicated with <nOrdinal>

IRdd.FieldName.summary: Retrieve a column name based on its ONE based column number.
IRdd.FieldName.param: The ONE based position of the column whose name you want to obtain.

IRdd.FilterText.summary: The filter condition as a string.

IRdd.Flush.summary: Flush the changes to the table, its indexes and memo file.

IRdd.ForceRel.summary: Force all pending relational seeks to be performed.

IRdd.Found.summary: The outcome of the last search operation.

IRdd.GetField.summary: Retrieve the field information for a column based on its ONE based column number.
IRdd.GetField.param: The ONE based position of the column whose info you want to obtain.
IRdd.GetField.returns: The Field information for the specified field.

IRdd.GetRec.summary: Retrieve the RDD's record buffer as array of bytes.
IRdd.GetRec.returns: Record buffer of the RDD. This is the original buffer, so changing this buffer may cause corruption !

IRdd.GetScope.returns: The current scope information for the RDD.

IRdd.GetValue.summary: Get a value for the specified column.
IRdd.GetValue.param: The ONE based position of the column whose value you want to obtain.
IRdd.GetValue.returns: The value of the specified field.

IRdd.GetValueFile.summary: Get the value for a column and write (export) it to an external file.
IRdd.GetValueFile.param: ONE based position for which the value should be retrieved.
Name of the file that needs to be written to.
IRdd.GetValueFile.returns: TRUE when the operation succeeded. Otherwise FALSE to indicate a failure.

IRdd.GetValueLength.summary: Get the length of the for the specified column.
IRdd.GetValueLength.param: ONE based position for which the length should be retrieved.
IRdd.GetValueLength.returns: The length of the value of the specified field.

IRdd.GoBottom.summary: Position the cursor to the last logical row.

IRdd.GoCold.summary: Write the contents of a work area's memory to the data store (usually a disk).

IRdd.GoHot.summary: Mark a data buffer as hot, indicating that it needs to be written to the data store.

IRdd.GoTo.summary: Position the cursor to a specific, physical row.
IRdd.GoTo.param: The ONE based row number of the new cursor position.

IRdd.GoToId.summary: Position the cursor to a specific, physical identity.
IRdd.GoToId.param: The ONE based row ID of the new cursor position.

IRdd.GoTop.summary: Position the cursor to the first logical row.

IRdd.HeaderLock.summary: Lock or unlock the header of a database file.
IRdd.HeaderLock.param: An enum value specifying the kind of lock to acquire.

IRdd.Info.summary: Retrieve and optionally change information about a work area.
IRdd.Info.param: If specified (not null), then this parameter is used to change the value of a setting.

IRdd.Lock.param: An object specifying the kind of lock to acquire.

IRdd.Open.param: object describing the file to open.

IRdd.OpenMemFile.param: object describing the file to open.

IRdd.OrderCondition.summary: Set the condition for the next Index Creation
IRdd.OrderCondition.param: An object containing information for the order condition.

IRdd.OrderCreate.summary: Create a new index or tag.
IRdd.OrderCreate.param: An object containing information for order creation.

IRdd.OrderDestroy.param: An object containing information about the order to remove.

IRdd.OrderInfo.param: An object containing information about the order to retrieve the info for.
Specifies the type of information to retrieve.
IRdd.OrderInfo.returns: The requested value for the specified index.

IRdd.OrderListAdd.summary: Open an index file and add to the list of open indexes for the current Workarea.
IRdd.OrderListAdd.param: An object containing information about the orderlist (file) to add.

IRdd.OrderListDelete.summary: Close an index file and remove it from the list of open indexes for the current Workarea.
IRdd.OrderListDelete.param: An object containing information about the orderlist (file) to delete.

IRdd.OrderListFocus.summary: Set focus to another index in the list open indexes for the current Workarea.
IRdd.OrderListFocus.param: An object containing information about the order to select.

IRdd.OrderListRebuild.summary: Rebuild all indexes for the current Workarea.

IRdd.Pack.summary: Physically remove rows marked for deletion.

IRdd.PutRec.summary: Replace the row at the current cursor position with the contents of a byte array.
IRdd.PutRec.param: The buffer containing the information that you want to write.
IRdd.PutRec.note: The buffer should have at least the # of bytes that matches the current record size.

IRdd.PutValue.summary: Write a value for a specified column
IRdd.PutValue.param: ONE based position for which the value should be written.
New value that needs to written to the table this column.

IRdd.PutValueFile.summary: Read (Import) a value from an external file and write it to the specified column.
IRdd.PutValueFile.param: ONE based position for which the value should be written.
Name of the file that needs to be read from.

IRdd.Recall.summary: Remove the deletion marker from the row at the current cursor position.

IRdd.RecId.summary: The row identifier at the current cursor position.

IRdd.RecInfo.summary: Retrieve and optionally change information about a row.
IRdd.RecInfo.param: If specified (not null), then this parameter is used to specify for which object to retrieve the info.
If specified (not null), then this parameter is used to change the value of a setting.

IRdd.RecNo.summary: The physical row identifier at the current cursor position.

IRdd.Refresh.summary: Discard all changes to the current record and reread the buffer from disk. When the current buffer is in Append mode then the server will move to the bottom of the file.

IRdd.RelArea.summary: Retrieve the logical number of a related work area.
IRdd.RelArea.param: The ONE based relation number for which to obtain the logical number.
IRdd.RelArea.returns: The area number of the relation.

IRdd.RelEval.summary: Evaluate a code block against the relation in a work area.
IRdd.RelEval.param: An object containing information about the relation

IRdd.RelText.summary: Retrieve the key expression of a relation.
IRdd.RelText.param: The ONE based relation number for which to obtain the key expression.
IRdd.RelText.returns: The key expression of the relation.

IRdd.Seek.summary: Perform a seek operation on the current selected index for the current Workarea.
IRdd.Seek.param: An object containing containing the necessary seek information.
IRdd.Seek.remarks: The result of the actial seek operation is stored in the Found property of the RDD and the EOF property.

IRdd.SetFieldExtent.summary: Set the Number of Fields the AddField Method will add.
IRdd.SetFieldExtent.param: Number of fields that will be added.

IRdd.SetRel.param: An object containing information about the relation

IRdd.Shared.summary: Is the current Workarea opened Shared?

IRdd.Skip.summary: Position the cursor relative to its current position.
IRdd.Skip.param: The number of rows to skip. If this argument is positive, the cursor moves forward (toward the end-of-file). If it is negative, the cursor moves backward (toward the beginning-of-file).

IRdd.SkipFilter.summary: Position the cursor, respecting scope and filter conditions.
