IRdd.SkipFilter.param: The number of rows to skip. If this argument is positive, the cursor moves forward (toward the end-of-file). If it is negative, the cursor moves backward (toward the beginning-of-file).

IRdd.SkipRaw.summary: Position the cursor regardless of scope and filter conditions.
IRdd.SkipRaw.param: The number of rows to skip. If this argument is positive, the cursor moves forward (toward the end-of-file). If it is negative, the cursor moves backward (toward the beginning-of-file).

IRdd.SkipScope.summary: Position the cursor relative to its current position within the current scope.
IRdd.SkipScope.param: The number of rows to skip. If this argument is positive, the cursor moves forward (toward the end-of-file). If it is negative, the cursor moves backward (toward the beginning-of-file).

IRdd.Sort.param: An object containing information about now to sort the table.

IRdd.SyncChildren.summary: Force relational movement in child work areas to synchronize them with the parent work area.

IRdd.Trans.summary: Copy one or more rows from one work area to another.
IRdd.Trans.param: An object containing information about the transfer of data.

IRdd.TransRec.summary: Copy a single row from one work area to another.
IRdd.TransRec.param: An object containing information about the transfer of data.

IRdd.UnLock.param: The row number to unlock. This argument is provided for drivers that support multiple row locks. By convention, a value of zero indicates that all rows should be unlocked.

IRdd.Zap.summary: Physically remove all rows from a table.

IsAlphaNum.summary: Determine if the leftmost character in a string is alphanumeric..
IsAlphaNum.returns: TRUE if the first character is either alphabetic or numeric otherwise FALSE.

Like.summary: Determine if a string matches a wildcard pattern (like the wildcard pattern for the DIR command in the OS).
Like.param: The wildcard to use. '*' matches 0 or more characters until the next non-wildcard character, '?' matches any character, all other characters must match exactly.
Like.remarks: This function is case INsensitive in all dialects except FoxPro. If you want to do a case sensitive compare in these dialects, use _Like()

LockMethod.Exclusive.summary: Lock a row, releasing currently locked rows.

LockMethod.File.summary: Lock a table, releasing locks currently held.

LockMethod.Multiple.summary: Lock a row, maintaining currently locked rows.

MacroCompilerErrorHandler.summary: You can register a function / method with this prototype in the RuntimeState to intercept errors in the macro compiler
MacroCompilerErrorHandler.param: Source of the macro that causes the error
MacroCompilerErrorHandler.returns: An object that implements ICodeblock, or NULL to tell the macro compiler to throw the error/
MacroCompilerErrorHandler.remarks: This delegate is called when the macro compiler encounters an error. The delegate can decide to return a codeblock that will be used instead of the original codeblock In theory you can 'patch' the cMacro and call the macro compiler again. However that may cause an other error, resulting in an endless loop.

MacroCompilerIncludeAssemblyInCache.summary: This delegate is used to decide if an assembly should be included in the type cache
MacroCompilerIncludeAssemblyInCache.param: Assembly object of the assembly being inspected
MacroCompilerIncludeAssemblyInCache.returns: TRUE when the types in the assembly need to be cached.

MacroCompilerResolveAmbiguousMatch.summary: This delegate is used to decide between 2 ambigous methods, functions or constructors
MacroCompilerResolveAmbiguousMatch.param: The types of the arguments that were passed to the method call
MacroCompilerResolveAmbiguousMatch.returns: The delegate should return 1 when it wants to use the first symbol and 2 when it wants to use the second symbol or 0 when it does not want to use either symbol

MemoRead.remarks: This function should NOT be used to read the contents of a binary file (such as a word document). Use MemoReadBinary() in stead .

MemoReadBinary.summary: Return the contents of a binary file as an array of bytes. Use this function in stead of MemoRead() to read the contents of a binary file.
MemoReadBinary.param: The name of the binary file to read from disk, including an optional drive, directory, and extension. SetDefault() and SetPath() settings are ignored; the Windows default is used unless you specify a drive and directory as part of the file name. No extension is assumed
MemoReadBinary.returns: The file as an array of bytes

MemoWritBinary.summary: Write binary data o a disk file. Use this function for binary files instead of MemoWrit(). This day may be read with MemoReadBinary().
MemoWritBinary.param: The name of the target disk file, including an optional drive, directory, and extension. SetDefault() and SetPath() settings are ignored; the Windows default is used unless you specify a drive and directory as part of the file name. No extension is assumed. If the file does not exist, it is created. If it exists, this function attempts to open the file in exclusive mode and, if successful, the file is overwritten without warning or error. If access is denied because, for example, another process is using the file, MemoWrit() returns FALSE and NetErr() is set to TRUE.
MemoWritBinary.returns: TRUE if the writing operation is successful; otherwise, FALSE

Oem2Ansi_2.summary: Convert an array of OEM characters to ANSI characters.
Oem2Ansi_2.param: A byte array that contains the string to convert

Oem2Ansi_3.param: The number of characters to convert

Oem2AnsiA.param: A byte array that contains the string to convert

PageBuffers.summary: This class was inspired by the DiskIO module in Visual Objects

Ptr2Bin.summary: Convert a intptr to a string

RddError.summary: Error subclass used by the RDD system.

RddFieldInfo.summary: Helper class for the RDD system to store field information

RddFieldInfo.Alias.summary: Alternative name, no length limit. This is the Caption for VFP fields

RddFieldInfo.BlankValue.summary: Return the blank (non null) value of the column.

RddFieldInfo.Caption.summary: Dynamic property for the Caption. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.ColumnName.summary: Dynamic property for the ColumnName. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.CopyValues.summary: Copy values from one object to another.
RddFieldInfo.CopyValues.remarks: Only the fields will be copied.

RddFieldInfo.ctor.param: Type, may also contain flags in the form of a colon follwed by N,0,B,+,Z,E,U
Length 'DBF style', so length in Buffer

RddFieldInfo.ctor_2.param: Length 'DBF style', so length in Buffer

RddFieldInfo.DefaultValue.summary: Dynamic property for the DefaultValue. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.Description.summary: Dynamic property for the Description. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.Flags.summary: Flags, such as Nullable, AutoIncrement, Binary etc.

RddFieldInfo.Format.summary: Dynamic property for the Format. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.InputMask.summary: Dynamic property for the InputMask. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.IsCompressed.summary: Is it a compressed field (not implemented yet) ?

RddFieldInfo.IsEncrypted.summary: Is it an encryped field (not implemented yet)?

RddFieldInfo.IsStandard.summary: Is it a standard Dbase 3 field (CDLMN) ?

RddFieldInfo.IsUnique.summary: Dynamic property for the IsUnique. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.IsVarLength.summary: Is it a variable length field ?

RddFieldInfo.IsVfp.summary: Is it a VFP extended field ?

RddFieldInfo.Offset.summary: Offset in the record buffer for DBF fields.

RddFieldInfo.Ordinal.summary: 1 based Ordinal position in the RDD.

RddFieldInfo.PrimaryKey.summary: Dynamic property for the PrimaryKey. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.Properties.summary: Dynamic list of optional properties, such as Caption, Description.
RddFieldInfo.Properties.remarks: These properties are used to store VFP specific properties and are read from the DBC file.

RddFieldInfo.RuleExpression.summary: Dynamic property for the RuleExpression. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.RuleText.summary: Dynamic property for the RuleText. Read from the VFP properties collection.

RddFieldInfo.SameType.summary: Check if two fields match in type, length and decimals.

RddFieldInfo.Validate.summary: Validate combinations of type, length and decimals.

RegisteredRDD.summary: This class is used to register RDD names with the matching System.Types

RegisteredRDD.Add.summary: Add a registration for a new RDD.
RegisteredRDD.Add.returns: FALSE when the RDD name is already registered, TRUE when the registration succeeded.

RegisteredRDD.Assembly.summary: Assembly object where the RDD is defined

RegisteredRDD.AssemblyName.summary: Name of the assembly where the RDD is defined.

RegisteredRDD.Find.summary: Locate an entry for a particular RDD name
RegisteredRDD.Find.returns: NULL when no RDD registration found.

RegisteredRDD.TypeName.summary: Fully qualified type name of the RDD

RegisterFileSearch.summary: Register Worker function for File Search API.
RegisterFileSearch.param: Function that implements the worker. Must implement the FileSearcher delegate, so take STRING parameter and return a STRING

RuntimeState.summary: Container Class that holds the XSharp Runtime state
RuntimeState.remarks:
Please note that unlike in most XBase products every thread has its own copy of the runtime state.
The runtime state from a new thread is a copy of the state of the main thread at that moment.

RuntimeState.AppModule.summary: The System.Reflection.Module for the main application.

RuntimeState.AutoLockMethod.summary: Delegate that describes the signature of the AutoLock methods

RuntimeState.AutoOpen.summary: The current AutoOpen setting (used by the RDD system).

RuntimeState.AutoOrder.summary: The current AutoOrder setting (used by the RDD system).

RuntimeState.AutoShareMode.summary: The current AutoShareMode setting (used by the RDD system).

RuntimeState.BreakLevel.summary: Current Break Level. Gets set by compiler generated code for BEGIN SEQUENCE .. END constructs.

RuntimeState.Century.summary: The current Century setting (used in DATE <-> STRING conversions).

RuntimeState.CollationMode.summary: The current Collation mode (used by the RDD system).
RuntimeState.CollationMode.remarks: Send an OnCollationChanged event when changed.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionAllowOldStyle.summary: The current compiler setting for the AllowOldStyleAssignments compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionAllowOldStyle.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionAZ.summary: The current compiler setting for the AZ compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionAZ.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionFOVF.summary: The current compiler setting for the FOVF compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionFOVF.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionFox2.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO13 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionFox2.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionOVF.summary: The current compiler setting for the OVF compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionOVF.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO10.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO10 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO10.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO11.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO11 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO11.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO12.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO12 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO12.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO13.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO13 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO13.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO14.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO12 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO14.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO4.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO4 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO4.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO6.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO6 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO6.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO7.summary: The current compiler setting for the VO7 compiler option.
RuntimeState.CompilerOptionVO7.value: The default value for this option is 'False'.

RuntimeState.CurrentWorkarea.summary: Gets / Sets the current Workarea number.

RuntimeState.DataSession.summary: The DataSession information for the current Thread.

RuntimeState.DateCountry.summary: The current DateCountry setting mode (used in DATE <-> STRING conversions).

RuntimeState.DateFormat.remarks:
This string should contain a combination of DD MM and either YY or YYYY characters.
For example DD-MM-YYYY for italian date format, MM/DD/YYYY for American date format or DD/MM/YYYY for British Date format. Note that all other characters except the four groups mentioned above are copied to the output string verbatim. This value is 'per thread'

RuntimeState.Decimals.summary: The default number of decimals for new FLOAT values that are created without explicit decimals.

RuntimeState.DecimalSep.summary: The default number of decimals for new FLOAT values that are created without explicit decimals.

RuntimeState.Deleted.summary: RDD Deleted Flag that determines whether to ignore or include records that are marked for deletion.

RuntimeState.DelimRDD.summary: Name of the RDD uses for DELIM operations.

RuntimeState.DialectChanged.summary: This event is invoked when the runtime dialect is changed.
RuntimeState.DialectChanged.remarks: Normally this will almost never happen. However inside our test suites this is quite common

RuntimeState.Digits.summary: The default number of digits for new FLOAT values that are created without explicit decimals

RuntimeState.DigitsFixed.summary: Logical setting that fixes the number of digits used to display numeric output.

RuntimeState.DosCodePage.summary: The 'DOS' Codepage. This gets read at startup from the OS().
RuntimeState.DosCodePage.remarks: Sends an OnCodePageChanged event when changed.

RuntimeState.DosEncoding.summary: The DOS Encoding. This is based on the corrent DOS Codepage.

RuntimeState.Eof.summary: Should text files, such as memo files be written with a closing EOF = chr(26) character.

RuntimeState.Eol.summary: End of line character to be used by the runtime. Defaults to CR + LF (13 + 13).

RuntimeState.Epoch.summary: Date Epoch value that determines how dates without century digits are interpreted.

RuntimeState.EpochCent.summary: Date Epoch Century value. This gets set by the SetEpoch() function to the century in which the Epoch year falls.

RuntimeState.EpochYear.summary: Date Epoch Year value. This gets set by the SetEpoch() function to the Epoch year % 100.

RuntimeState.Exact.summary: String comparison Exact flag that determines how comparisons with the single '=' characters should be done.

RuntimeState.FileError.summary: The last File IO error number

RuntimeState.Fixed.summary: Logical setting that fixes the number of decimal digits used to display numbers.

RuntimeState.FloatDelta.summary: Numeric value that controls the precision of Float comparisons.

RuntimeState.GetInstance.summary: Retrieve the runtime state for the current thread

RuntimeState.HPLocking.summary: The setting that determines whether to use the High Performance (HP) locking schema for newly created .NTX files

RuntimeState.LastFound.summary: The last file found with File(). This is the name that FPathName() returns.

RuntimeState.LastRddError.summary: Last error that occurred in the RDD subsystem.

RuntimeState.LockTries.summary: Number of tries that were done when the last lock operation failed.

remarks.note: This value is NOT 'per thread' but global for all threads.

RuntimeState.MacroCompilerErrorHandler.summary: You can register an error handler that will be called when the macrocompiler encounters an error

RuntimeState.MacroCompilerIncludeAssembl.summary: This property allows you to override the mechanism that decided if the types in an assembly should be cached The default behavior is that every assembly that is found is cached.

remarks.note: This value is NOT 'per thread' but global for all threads.

doc.RuntimeState.Name: Retrieve state name String value, such as "State for Thread 123"IDb

RuntimeState.NetErr.summary: Did the last RDD operation cause a Network Error ?

RuntimeState.NewIndexLock.summary: The setting that determines whether to use the new locking offset of -1 (0xFFFFFFFF) for .NTX files.

RuntimeState.NoMethod.summary: The name of the method that was called in the last late bound method call.

RuntimeState.NullDateString.summary: A cached copy of the string that is returned for empty dates, matching the current DateFormat

RuntimeState.OnCodePageChanged.summary: This event is thrown when one of the codepages of the runtimestate is changed
RuntimeState.OnCodePageChanged.remarks: Clients can refresh cached information by registering to this event

RuntimeState.OnCollationChanged.summary: This event is thrown when the collation of the runtimestate is changed
RuntimeState.OnCollationChanged.remarks: Clients can refresh cached information by registering to this event

RuntimeState.ScriptCompiler.summary: Active Script compiler. Gets assigned from the ExecScriptFast() function.

remarks.note: This value is NOT 'per thread' but global for all threads.

RuntimeState.SetDataSession.summary: This method can be used to switch the active DataSession in the runtime state.
RuntimeState.SetDataSession.param: The datasession that needs to be set as the new active datasession
RuntimeState.SetDataSession.remarks: This sets the DataSession in the current thread.

RuntimeState.Settings.summary: The dictionary that stores most of the settings in the runtime state. The key to the index is the number from the Set Enum

RuntimeState.StateChanged.summary: This event is invoked when the runtime state is changed.

overloads.summary: Compare 2 strings respecting the runtime string comparison rules.

RuntimeState.StringCompare.remarks:
The StringCompare method takes into account:
- the setting of SetExact()
- the setting of VO13 of the main app
- the setting of Collation mode
This method respects the current setting of SetCollation():
- When the current collationmode is Clipper or Windows then a Unicode - Ansi conversions will be performed. The Clipper collation uses the current DOS codepage and the Windows collation the current Windows codepage.
- When the current collationmode is Unicode or Ordinal then the original strings will be compared.

RuntimeState.StringCompare_2.summary: Compare 2 byte arrays respecting the runtime string comparison rules.
RuntimeState.StringCompare_2.remarks:
This method works on BYTE arrays and is used by the RDD system.
This method respects the current setting of SetCollation():
- When the current collationmode is Clipper or Windows then no Ansi - Unicode conversions will be done.The comparisons will be done on the byte arrays.
- When the current collationmode is Unicode or Ordinal then the byte arrays will be converted to Unicode before the comparison is executed.

RuntimeState.StringCompareCollation.remarks: This method only checks for SetCollation and does not check fot SetExact() or the VO13 setting

RuntimeState.ToString.returns: String value, such as "State for Thread 123"

RuntimeState.Unique.summary: Should indexes by default be created with the 'Unique setting'.

RuntimeState.WinCodePage.summary: The Windows Codepage. This gets read at startup from the OS().
RuntimeState.WinCodePage.remarks: Sends an OnCodePageChanged event when changed.

RuntimeState.WinEncoding.summary: The Windows Encoding. This is based on the corrent Win Codepage.

RuntimeState.Workareas.summary: The Workarea information for the current Thread.
RuntimeState.Workareas.remarks: This property is a backward compatible alias for the DataSession property

SetCollation_2.returns: The current setting, either "Windows" (the default), "Clipper", "Unicode" or "Ordinal"
SetCollation_2.param: The collation mode to use. The available modes are "Windows" (the default), "Clipper", "Unicode" and "Ordinal".

SetErrorLogFile.summary: Get the name of the current errorlog file
SetErrorLogFile.returns: Current name of the error log file.

SetErrorLogFile_2.summary: Set the name of the current errorlog file
SetErrorLogFile_2.param: New name of error log file
SetErrorLogFile_2.returns: Previous name of the error log file.

SetErrorStackVOFormat.summary: This function allows you to enable or disable the VO compatible Errorstack format.
SetErrorStackVOFormat.param: Specify TRUE to enable the new format.
SetErrorStackVOFormat.returns: The current setting for the ErrorStack format.
SetErrorStackVOFormat.remarks: The default setting for the format is based on the dialect of the main application. When the main dialect is VO or Vulcan then the VO format is used. Otherwise the normal .Net format is used.

SetInternational.remarks:
SetInternational() allows XSharp apps to operate in different international modes. The "CLIPPER" mode is provided for compatibility with CA-Clipper applications and uses an internationalization routine defined in the nation module.
The "Windows" mode uses international services provided by Windows. When you set this mode several settings will be changed
Setting Initial value in CLIPPER mode
SetAmExt Empty String
SetPmExt Empty String
SetAmPm FALSE (24 hour format)
SetCentury FALSE
SetDateCountry American (1)
SetDateFormat mm/dd/yy
SetDecimal 2
SetDecimalSep Period (.)
SetThousandSep Comma (,)
SetTimeSep Colon(:)

SetMacroCompiler.summary: Set the type of the class that must be used to compile macros
SetMacroCompiler.param: The type of the class that implements the macro compiler. This type MUST implement IMacroCompiler.
SetMacroCompiler.returns: The type of the previously defined MacroCompiler. This may be NULL if no type has been set yet and no macros have been compiled.

SetMacroCompiler_2.summary: Set the class that must be used to compile macros
SetMacroCompiler_2.param: The object that implements the macro compiler.
SetMacroCompiler_2.returns: The previously defined MacroCompiler. This may be NULL if no compiler has been set yet and no macros have been compiled.

SetMacroDuplicatesResolver.summary: Set the delegate that may be used to decide which symbol to call for ambiguous symbols

SetNatDLL.remarks:
In Visual Objects the nation DLL is an external DLL. For X# there are no separate nation DLL. The nation support code is integrated in XSharp.Core.DLL. For compatibility you can still use the SetNatDLL() function to switch to another nation module.
The available nation modules are (just like in Visual Objects):
"BRAZIL", "CROATIA", "CZECH852", "CZECH895", "DANISH", "DUTCH", "FINNISH", "FRENCH", "GENERIC", "GERMAN", "GERMAN2", "HUNG852", "HUNGCWI", "ITALIAN", "NORWEGN", "POL-ISO", "POL-MAZ", "POL852", "PORT850", "PORT860", "ROMANIA", "RUSSIAN", "SERBIA", "SL-W-95", "SL-W-AS7", "SL-W-EE", "SLOV852", "SLOV895", "SPANISH", "SWEDISH", "TURKISH", "UK"
You may specify a DLL name with full path. The Runtime will strip the path and extension from the file name and will look for one of the names listed above.
The nation DLL name is NOT case sensitive.

SetPathArray.summary: Return the Path array that is used by the File() function to locate files outside of the current directory. This is a combination of the SetDefault() and SetPath() variables. This may be null if the file function has never been called or never been called for files outside of the current directory.

SetSpace.summary: Return the setting that determines whether a space is displayed between fields or expressions when you use the ? or ?? command.

SetSpace_2.summary: Change the setting that determines whether a space is displayed between fields or expressions when you use the ? or ?? command.

Severity.summary: Error code Enum that matches the XSharp Error Severity codesFDesc
Severity.note: These enums are also available as DEFINES and can therefore also be used without the "Severity." prefix.

SLen.remarks: The compiler will replace calls to SLen() as much as possible with a direct call to the Length property of a string.

SQLProperty.summary: Enum that matches the various FoxPro Sql properties, used in SqlGetProp() and SqlSetProp()

SQLProperty.Asynchronous.summary: Specifies whether result sets are returned synchronously.

SQLProperty.BatchMode.summary: Specifies whether SqlExec( ) returns result sets all at once (True (.T.), the default), or individually with SqlMoreResults( ) (False (.F.)).

SQLProperty.ConnectBusy.summary: Contains True (.T.) if a shared connection is busy; otherwise contains False (.F.).

SQLProperty.ConnectTimeOut.summary: Specifies the time to wait (in seconds) before returning a connection time-out error. If you specify 0, the wait is indefinite and a time-out error is never returned. ConnectTimeOut can be 0 to 600. The default is 15.

SQLProperty.DataSource.summary: The name of the data source as defined in the ODBC.INI file.

SQLProperty.DisconnectRollback.summary: Specifies if a pending transaction is committed or rolled back when SqlDisconnect( ) is called for the last connection handle.

SQLProperty.DispLogin.summary: Contains a numeric value that determines when the ODBC Login dialog box is displayed.

SQLProperty.DispWarnings.summary: Specifies if error messages are displayed (True (.T.)) or are not displayed (False (.F.), the default).

SQLProperty.IdleTimeout.summary: The idle timeout interval in minutes. Active connections are deactivated after the specified time interval. The default value is 0 (wait indefinitely).

SQLProperty.NativeCommand.summary: The native commandtext in the SQLStatement object.

SQLProperty.ODBChdbc.summary: The internal ODBC connection, which may be used by external code to call ODBC.
SQLProperty.ODBChdbc.remarks: In X# this does not return the connection handle but the DbConnection object !

SQLProperty.ODBChstmt.summary: The internal ODBC statement , which may be used by external code to call ODBC.
SQLProperty.ODBChstmt.remarks: In X# this does not return the statement handle but the DbCommand object !

SQLProperty.PacketSize.summary: The size of the network packet used by the connection. Adjusting this value can improve performance. The default value is 4096 bytes (4K).

SQLProperty.QueryTimeOut.summary: Specifies the time to wait (in seconds) before returning a general time-out error. If you specify 0 (the default), the wait is indefinite and a time-out error is never returned. QueryTimeOut can be 0 to 600.

SQLProperty.Shared.summary: Specifies whether the underlying connection is a shared connection (True (.T.)), or not (False (.F.)).

SQLProperty.Transactions.summary: Contains a numeric value that determines how the connection manages transactions on the remote table.

SQLProperty.WaitTime.summary: The amount of time in milliseconds that elapses before Visual FoxPro checks if the SQL statement has completed executing. The default is 100 milliseconds.

StateChangedEventArgs.summary: Arguments that are sent to StateChanged event handlers

SToDt.summary: Convert an Date string to DateTime

String2FAttr.param: One or more of the following constants or strings: ADHSRV

Subcodes.summary: Error code Enum that matches the XSharp Error Sub Codes
Subcodes.note: These enums are also available as DEFINES and can therefore also be used without the "Subcodes." prefix.

SwapInt64.summary: Exchange the right and left halves of a Int64

SystemTypeToUsualType.summary: Calculate the Usual Type for a System Type
SystemTypeToUsualType.remarks: You can use this function in assemblies that do not have a reference to XSharp.RT to determine the type of a value
SystemTypeToUsualType.example:
This example converts a symbol that is passed inside an object to a string
if SystemTypeToUsualType(oOrder:GetType()) == __UsualType.Symbol oOrder := oOrder:ToString() endif
SystemTypeToUsualType.param: The type of the value to check

TypeString.summary: Return a descriptive name (in upper case) for a X# (Usual) Type

UseBufferedFileStream.summary: Control the buffered File IO setting for the runtime
UseBufferedFileStream.remarks:
The default behavior for X# 2.7 and later is to use Buffered Filestreams for Exclusive disk io.
With this function you can switch that behavior on and off.
UseBufferedFileStream.param: Should the buffer file IO be used ?
UseBufferedFileStream.returns: Previous setting for the buffered file IO

UseBufferedFileStream_2.summary: Retrieve the buffered File IO setting for the runtime
UseBufferedFileStream_2.returns: Current setting for the buffered file IO

UUDecodeLine.summary: This function is not implemented yet

UUEncFile.summary: This function is not implemented yet

UUEncLine.summary: This function is not implemented yet

VoStructAttribute.Size.summary: Total size of the VOSTRUCT or UNION.

Workarea._EvalResult.summary: Result of the last Block evaluation.

Workarea._Flush.summary: Should the file be flushed (it is dirty) ?

Workarea._hFile.summary: File handle of the current file

Workarea._MaxFieldNameLength.summary: Maximum fieldname length (Advantage supports field names > 10 characters)

Workarea.FileName.summary: File name of the main file

Workarea.GetField.summary: Return the (1 based) RddFieldInfo object for the specified field
Workarea.GetField.returns: FieldInfo object or NULL when the index is not valid

Workarea.OpenInfo.summary: File information from the Open/Create methods

Workarea.SetFieldExtent.summary: Set the Number of Fields the AddField Method will add.

Workareas.summary: Class that contains the list of open Workareas. Each thread will have its own list.

Workareas.CloseAll.summary: Close All RDDs referenced by this Workarea list
Workareas.CloseAll.returns: TRUE when all areas were closed succesfully. When one or more areas failed to close then FALSE is returned.

Workareas.CloseArea.summary: Close area with 1 based Workarea number
Workareas.CloseArea.param: 1 based Area number for which to find the alias
Workareas.CloseArea.returns: TRUE when the area was closed succesfully.

Workareas.CloseArea_2.summary: Close the area where this RDD object is used.
Workareas.CloseArea_2.param: Object of te RDD that needs to be closed.
Workareas.CloseArea_2.returns: TRUE when the area was closed.
Workareas.CloseArea_2.remarks: This will close the area, even when the method is called from another thread.

Workareas.CommitAll.summary: Commit changes in all workares in this Workarea list
Workareas.CommitAll.returns: TRUE when all areas were committed succesfully. When one or more areas failed to commit then FALSE is returned.

Workareas.FindAlias.summary: Return 1 based Workarea Number for Alias or 0 when no found
Workareas.FindAlias.param: Alias of area to look for. Case INsensitive
Workareas.FindAlias.returns: Area number of an the table with the given alias.

Workareas.FindEmptyArea.param: Do we start to search from the start (TRUE) or the end (FALSE)
Workareas.FindEmptyArea.returns: Area number of an area where no table is open.

Workareas.GetAlias.summary: Get Alias for 1 based Workarea Number
Workareas.GetAlias.param: 1 based Area number for which to find the alias
Workareas.GetAlias.returns: Alias or an empty string when there is no table open in the area

Workareas.GetRDD.summary: Get RDD object for 1 based Workarea Number

Workareas.SetArea.summary: Set RDD object and ALias for 1 based Workarea Number

Workareas.UnLockAll.summary: Unlock All RDDs referenced by this Workarea list

WrappedException.summary: Helper class for the BREAK statement

WrappedException.Value.summary: The property that is passed by the BREAK statement

XsBufferedFileStream.summary: This class performs buffered IO to files opened exclusively

XsBufferedFileStream.Close.remarks: This method overrides the normal behavior of the FileStream class and flushes the cached data to disk before calling the parent Close() method.

XsBufferedFileStream.Flush.remarks: This method overrides the normal behavior of the FileStream class and flushes the cached data to disk before calling the parent Flush() method.

XsBufferedFileStream.Read.remarks: This method overrides the normal behavior of the FileStream class and reads the data from an inmemory cache, when possible

XsBufferedFileStream.Write.remarks: This method overrides the normal behavior of the FileStream class and writed the data to an inmemory cache, when possible

XsBufferedFileStream.WriteByte.remarks: This method overrides the normal behavior of the FileStream class and writed the data to an inmemory cache, when possible

XsBufferedFileStream.XRead.summary: Bypass the buffered Read mechanism and call the original Read() method in the base class

XsBufferedFileStream.XWrite.summary: Bypass the buffered Write mechanism and call the original Write() method in the base class

XsFileStream.summary: Special Filestream class that sets the runtime IO Errorstate when an error occurs.

XsFileStream.CreateFileStream.summary: Create a XsFileStream object on Windows and a normal FileStream object on other OS-es

XsFileStream.FileName.summary: The name of the file opened in the stream.

XSharp.DialectChanged.summary: Delegate used for the DialectChanged Event handler

XSharp.FileSearcher.summary: This delegate is used when you want to implement your own function for the logic behind the File() function. You can register your own FileSearcher by calling the RegisterFileSearch() function.

XSharp.StateChanged.summary: Delegate used for the StateChanged Event handler

XSharpDialect.summary: This enum lists the various dialects possible in XSharp. The enum MUST match the dialect values inside the compiler

XsWin32FileStream.summary: This class is used for Shared diskaccess on Windows.
XsWin32FileStream.remarks: The class bypasses some of the default methods in the FileStraem class and directly uses calls to OS functions to make sure that changes made by other users are visible and not hidden because of caching in the .Net FileStream class.

XsWin32FileStream.Flush.remarks: This method calls the Windows FlushFileBuffers() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Length.remarks: This method calls the Windows GetFileSize() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Lock.remarks: This method calls the Windows LockFile() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Read.remarks: This method calls the Windows ReadFile() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Seek.remarks: This method calls the Windows SetFilePointerEx() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.SetLength.remarks: This method calls the Windows SetEndOfFile() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Unlock.remarks: This method calls the Windows UnlockFile() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.Write.remarks: This method calls the Windows WriteFile() function directly.

XsWin32FileStream.WriteByte.remarks: This method calls the Windows WriteFile() function directly.
